title: troximon laciniatum
intro: Troximon laciniatum, also known as the laciniated troximon or cut-leaved troximon, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and its name comes from the Greek words "trochos" meaning wheel and "monos" meaning solitary, referring to the plant's solitary flower head. The medicinal properties of Troximon laciniatum include its ability to reduce inflammation and its antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, and it can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, which is often used to treat a range of health issues.
